hook.question: See how you think before you delegate.

trackIntro.title: How This Deck Flows
trackIntro.subtitle: Three pillars guide this overview: what Thinkprint captures, the full extraction lifecycle, and how it relates to JDVP.

extraction.title: Logic Extraction
extraction.description: Capture logic structure from conversations — not memory, but how you think.

calibration.title: Lifecycle & Artifacts
calibration.description: Six-step pipeline from extraction to publish, producing versioned mode packages.

connection.title: Independent, Connectable
connection.description: Operates standalone on any agent. Optional JDVP synergy adds measurement.

trackIntro.conclusion: Thinkprint turns implicit thinking habits into explicit, inspectable logic structures — not a fixed label, but an evolving mode.

point3.title: Extraction Lifecycle
point3.subtitle: Extract → Calibrate → Deepen → Freeze → Evaluate → Publish

point5.title: JDVP Relationship
point5.subtitle: Independent by design, connectable by choice

point1.title: What Thinkprint Is
point1.subtitle: Thinkprint captures your logic structure — not what you remember.

toy.title: Logic-Structure Extractor
toy.description: Externalize personal reasoning patterns into inspectable, shareable modes.

reflection.title: Logic over Identity
reflection.description: A reasoning configuration, not a personality claim. Editable by default, versioned, comparable.

declaration.title: Editable Reasoning Profile
declaration.description: Decision priorities, confidence thresholds, delegation boundaries, tradeoff patterns, and revision triggers.

point2.title: What Thinkprint Is Not
point2.subtitle: Clearing up what Thinkprint deliberately avoids.

profiling.title: Not Commercial Profiling
profiling.description: No behavioral targeting or data monetization — purely self-directed exploration.

psychology.title: Not Memory Replication
psychology.description: Captures logic structure, not memories or lived experiences.

alignment.title: Not AI Alignment Control
alignment.description: A personal reasoning tool, not a system for controlling AI behavior.

cloning.title: Not Human Duplication
cloning.description: Extracts logic patterns — doesn't attempt to clone or replicate a person.

point3.title: Extraction Lifecycle
point3.subtitle: Six steps from conversation to publishable mode package.

extract.title: Extract
extract.description: Derive initial reasoning candidates from dialogue and events.

calibrate.title: Calibrate
calibrate.description: Resolve thresholds and preferences via targeted probes.

freeze.title: Freeze
freeze.description: Produce immutable version snapshot and artifact set.

evaluate.title: Evaluate
evaluate.description: Run benchmark scenarios and produce scorecard.

point4.title: Mode Package
point4.subtitle: Each versioned mode produces six canonical artifacts.

declaration.title: thinkprint.md
declaration.description: Human-readable policy: reasoning axes, decision priorities, delegation rules, risk boundaries.

prompt.title: agent_system_prompt.txt
prompt.description: Runtime instruction layer injected into agent system context to activate reasoning mode.

metadata.title: metadata.json
metadata.description: Version, extraction method, overall confidence, evaluation reference, and governance flags.

manifest.title: manifest.json
manifest.description: Mode package structure, integrity hashes, compatibility matrix, and license.

scenarios.title: scenario_pack.json
scenarios.description: Benchmark scenarios for testing mode consistency and delegation alignment.

evaluation.title: evaluation_report.json
evaluation.description: Scenario coverage, consistency score, risk violations, and publish readiness.

point5.title: JDVP Relationship
point5.subtitle: Thinkprint operates independently — no JDVP required. When connected, experimentation becomes measurable.

independence.title: Independent by Design
independence.description: Operates on any agent or human with reasoning behavior. Does not require JDVP or Meta Governance.

synergy.title: Optional Synergy
synergy.description: When combined with JDVP telemetry, reasoning drift becomes trackable and experimentation gains measurement.

future.title: Future Integration
future.description: JDVP drift metrics feed into Thinkprint mode evaluation — always as an optional extension.

summary.title: Your Thinking, Externalized

point1.label: What It Is:
point1.text: A protocol for extracting, versioning, and sharing logic structures as inspectable modes.

point2.label: How It Works:
point2.text: Six-step lifecycle: Extract → Calibrate → Deepen → Freeze → Evaluate → Publish.

point3.label: Why It Matters:
point3.text: Reasoning becomes explicit, portable, and — when connected with JDVP — measurable.

summary.tagline: Extract your reasoning patterns into inspectable, shareable modes.
